originalAlarm.description: `buffer` could be null (null value originating from line 39) and is dereferenced.

model:
(define-fun CONFIG_KPROBES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_KGDB () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Known VBDB bug for 221ac329e93.c at L37 (*buffer = a, ERROR). When KGDB=T (or XMON=T or BDI_SWITCH=T), allocate_buffer() is a macro using malloc(4092) which can return NULL. When KPROBES=T, *buffer = a at L37 dereferences possibly-null buffer — genuine null dereference. Known bug. The true PC should be KPROBES=T AND (KGDB=T OR XMON=T OR BDI_SWITCH=T). The reported PC {KGDB=T, KPROBES=T} is a subset, missing XMON=T and BDI_SWITCH=T configs. Over-constrained.
